A weekly audio discussion with Dr. Ivan Misner, the Founder and Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, the world's largest business networking organization.

Episode 731: Online and Hybrid Chapters

November 3, 2021 by Ivan Misner 10 Comments

Podcast: Download

Subscribe: Apple Podcasts | RSS | Subscription Options

Print This Episode

Synopsis

By every measurement, BNI has flourished through the pandemic thanks to BNI online™. Now, as of October 2021, all BNI chapters are eligible to become Hybrid Chapters. Sam Spuhler, product manager for BNI online™, joins Dr. Misner to explain how Hybrid Chapters work.

When your chapter elects to become a Hybrid Chapter, the first meeting of the month takes place in person. The rest of the month, the chapter meets online.

51% of BNI members surveyed in July of 2021 preferred a hybrid meeting format. Another 16% of Members prefer to stay with BNI online™ permanently.

Hybrid Chapters are an option. If your chapter wants to continue as an online-only chapter, you can do that. If your chapter is in an area where it’s safe to meet in person every week, you can do that. But if you want both the connection of in-person meetings and the convenience of BNI online™, you can become a Hybrid Chapter.

Read the transcript below for some BNI online™ testimonials from BNI members.

Complete Transcript of Episode 731

Priscilla:
Hello everybody, and welcome back to The Official BNI Podcast. I’m Priscilla Rice, and I’m coming to you from Live Oak Recording Studio in Berkeley, California. And I’m joined on the phone today by the Founder and the Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, Dr. Ivan Misner. Hello Ivan, how are you and where are you?

Ivan:
I have been Zooming around so many places, I can’t list them all. But I’ve done a live event in California with Steve Farber by Zoom, BNI Korea National Conference and other national conferences. And I’m headed to Necker Island again to meet with Richard Branson. And I hope to come back with some amazing stories, not too long from now.

Priscilla:
Great.

Ivan:
So it will be fun, and it’ll be very interesting as it has been in the past. Today, we have a guest on the show, and his name is Sam Spuhler. Sam is the Product Manager for BNI online™ for BNI Global. He has been involved with BNI Online since its inception in March 2020. And actually a little bit before that with some of the countries in Asia but globally, it expanded in March. He helped chapters around the world transition to the online meeting format at the start of the pandemic and has continued to provide day-to-day customer support to ensure a quality meeting experience. He conducts customer research and manages projects geared toward driving BNI innovation and increasing member value. I can’t think of anyone better to have on to talk about BNI online™ and Hybrid Chapters. Sam, welcome to BNI Podcast.

Sam:
Thank you very much, Ivan, for that introduction. It is fantastic to be here.

Ivan:
It’s great having you on. Talk a little bit about BNI online™ and why you think it is effective.

Sam:
Absolutely. So, you know, of course, BNI online™ was formed out of necessity after a few years of the organization recognizing the potential power of it for the future of BNI. But of course, you know, through the pandemic, we were able to enjoy a lot of success in a lot of ways using BNI online™ exclusively as the meeting format platform. And, you know, as we are now a year and a half into BNI online’s life, we of course have kept a very close eye on some of the KPIs, some of the metrics that drive our business.

Ivan:
And KPIs, for those of you who don’t know, is a Key Performance Indicator, the things that drive our business, as Sam just said.

Sam:
Yes, thank you. And, you know, so metrics such as overall membership, and member retention, member NPS scores, these are all metrics that we follow closely, of course.

Ivan:
NPS score is Net Promoter Score. That’s the happiness score, basically, are you happier or satisfied with what you’re getting? NPS.

Sam:
Yes, absolutely. And that’s, of course, something that every business owner should be keeping an eye on with their customer base. So all of these metrics that are listed, we’ve enjoyed an increase in them using BNI online™ largely as the operating engine. Other key metrics to the BNI model, of course, Thank You For Closed Business, and referrals. You know, they saw a small decrease at the start of the pandemic, of course, it’s a very large business disruption that took place last year and they quickly returned to those pre-pandemic levels, both Thank You For Closed Business and referrals, during this BNI online™ era. So, you know, in the last 12 months, BNI has generated $18.3 billion US dollars in Thank You For Closed Business.

Ivan:
That’s amazing.

Sam:
And again, using BNI online™ largely as the operating engine, so we’re quite proud of that.

Ivan:
Yeah, I mean, I get it with Members. You know, I created an in-person networking organization. And I get it that the online has some issues. But I ask everyone to think about this. What if this had happened 20 years ago? What if it had happened 30 years ago? There was no technology like this. We would virtually all be out of business. I mean, every one of our Members would be struggling, even more than many of them are. Either that, or we would be going out and about, and we’d have many more people ill and die.

And so yeah, you know, maybe the online is not quite as good as that handshake. But there are benefits. And I think you’re going to talk about some of those. But the alternative, the fact that this technology exists, is a blessing. And I had someone say to me not long ago, Sam, that she told the member who was whining about, she said, whining about “Zoom fatigue”. And she said “Buck up, Buttercup. You know, you could be out of business.” This is fantastic that we can do business online. Now, having said that, we do have chapters in areas that are gradually becoming safer. And there’s hybrid meetings that are taking place. And that’s what I know the Members want to hear about.

Sam:
Yes, absolutely. So what we do as a product team here at BNI, is we are very passionate about listening to our Members, we conduct a lot of customer research, a lot of surveys. And one thing that we found through a variety of surveys over the course of this year is there is a strong appetite for hybrid chapters, as you referenced Ivan. So, you know, we have officially launched this offering for chapters around the world as of October of 2021.

Ivan:
Is this for all chapters or just select chapters?

Sam:
So all chapters as of October 2021 are able to participate in the hybrid format. And what we define the hybrid format as, again, after a lot of extensive research, is that the first meeting of the month takes place in person. So the first week of the month meeting is in person. You enjoy your handshakes and your coffee at your venues as you usually would. And then the rest of the meetings of the month, take place using BNI online™. So the Members can enjoy that camaraderie, those handshakes, that building relationships that we’ve all known to love through the in person, traditional meeting setting. But also be able to enjoy the flexibility that a lot of Members prefer with BNI online™ too. And, you know, we really recognize that members want that ability to both build strong relationships, while not sacrificing a time savings.

Ivan:
Can I add something? Because I know there are some Members chomping at the bit here. We’re not saying that you have to go hybrid, we’re saying you can go hybrid. So if chapters, if it’s safe from a health perspective to meet in-person, you can continue to meet in-person. We have over 700 chapters, as of right now that started online, they’ve never met in person, they’re likely to not want to go to hybrid. So meeting online is okay, meeting in-person is okay. But I love the hybrid idea. And that’s not only okay, I think it’s a great idea.

Sam:
Yeah, and again, we listen to our Members, we follow what the data says. And 51% of Members that we surveyed in July of 2021, expressed that they preferred a hybrid meeting format that we’ve just discussed here. Another 16% of Members prefer BNI online™ permanently. So in my role and our team, the findings there is really exciting.

Ivan:
Yeah, that’s 2/3 of the membership wanted online or a hybrid.

Sam:
Yep.

Ivan:
What a change over the last couple years.

Sam:
I know and we’ll continue to poll our members and continue to be at the forefront of understanding what it is that they want. And, you know, our team will go act on that to provide a valuable experience.

Ivan:
So what are some of the reasons why some people prefer online?

Sam:
Yeah, through the surveys, we learned that this saves time and money for franchisees, for Directors, for Members. You’re not having to battle a commute or traffic jams or anything once a week. Of course, the flexibility that the offering has, I know you’ve expressed that you’ve been traveling around, as it is safe to do so, yourself, Ivan. And you could leverage BNI online™ to tune into your chapter meetings from wherever you are. And you know, it’s just become really extremely efficient with time and costs ultimately. And we’ve seen in some areas that inviting visitors is more effective, because the visitor themselves doesn’t have as much of a travel commitment. So these are a few reasons why many Members do prefer online, and we will continue to explore that.

Ivan:
So let’s talk for a moment, we’ve got another minute or so about the hybrid meetings. To me, it’s the best of both worlds. Because one of the things that I didn’t like about Zoom meetings or about online meetings is that you don’t see people 3-dimensionally. You don’t see all their body language, you can’t shake hands, in-person, you might not be able to shake hands right now either. But, you know, at some point, we’ll be back to that, and it’s just not as personal. So getting, you know, having one of those a month, to me, fills that void. And then the simplicity and the ease of not having to, you know, get up, prepare yourself professionally to get in a car and then drive whatever distance to a meeting, it just saves a ton of time. And so that combination to me is such a win-win. And it’s something that we couldn’t have done 10 or 20 years ago.

Sam:
Absolutely, it is definitely that best of both worlds. I always say that the in-person format is great for those that like business cards and handshakes, and the online format is great for those that would like to leverage the power of networking from their home or elsewhere, you know, anywhere that their busy life takes them. So it’s really an exciting offering that we are really proud of and really think will have a positive impact on the future of BNI.

Ivan:
Well, I know you have a lot of Member testimonials about the online and hybrid. Read two of them to us here, and then I’ll put the rest of them in the transcript so everybody will have that. Give us the first two that you’d like to share.

Sam:
Yeah, fantastic. So I’ll just read verbatim here: “Having virtual meetings is saving me about eight hours a month between getting ready, and driving time. I’d rather put that time into One-to-Ones.” And this was a Member in BNI India.

Ivan:
Brilliant.

Sam:
Absolutely. Next one: “I’ve been a member for 20+ years, and by far, meeting online has become extremely efficient with time and costs. The meeting runs smoothly and effectively. And it is also convenient to invite visitors.” That was from a [BNI] Member here in the USA.

Ivan:
And I’ll put a few more here in the transcript; one from Africa, UK, Canada, so you’ll get some from around the world.

“Online meetings are convenient and saves time and money.”

Member in BNI Kenya

“I’m fed up with wasting time in a car. If I want to have a 1-2-1 in person or socialise, then I can still do so.”

Member in BNI UK

“Since we have moved online, our attendance has been very consistent, the number of subs have dramatically decreased, and the number of visitors has increased.”

– Member in BNI Canada

“It just makes more sense to me both in saving time and money. Also, I think the real power of BNI is to be able to visit chapters worldwide via the online format…”

Member in BNI Canada

Ivan:
Sam, any closing thought before we wrap up?

Sam:
No. We’re just excited to continue to innovate and drive BNI forward. And, as always, Ivan, thank you for everything that you have done to build this fantastic and powerful network. We wouldn’t be here without you.

Ivan:
Oh, thank you so much, my pleasure. And everyone, I know these are challenging times. Those of us who know how, and are willing to pivot, will be successful and will get through these crazy times that we live in. That’s it for today. Sam, thank you so much. Let me turn it back over to you, Priscilla.

Priscilla:
Okay, that’s great. Thank you both. That was great information. This podcast is sponsored by www.misneraudioprograms.com. These audio programs will provide you with the tools and the inspiration to powerfully enhance your BNI experience and help you boost your business. So check out the great material available to you at www.misneraudioprograms.com, and use the promo code Ivan50 for 50% off of all the audio programs [this code is good for a limited time]. And all of the proceeds are going to go to the BNI Foundation.

Thank you so much for listening. This is Priscilla Rice, and we look forward to having you join us again next week for another exciting episode of The Official BNI Podcast.

ShareTweetLinkedinWhatsappSaveSendFlipboard

Special Guest Sam Spuhler Posted in Online Networking, Special Guests Tagged with BNI online™, Hybrid Chapters

You may also like...

Comments

    Solutions-focused comments are welcome. Dr. Misner reads every comment personally.

    All comments are moderated before posting. Profanity, personal attacks, and sales pitches will be deleted.

  1. Will Bond says

    November 4, 2021 at 7:29 am

    I’m a member of BNI, and I voted for the hybrid option, but I completely misunderstood the definition of hybrid. I wonder if many others did the same and skewed your survey results.

    A hybrid meeting to me is one where there is an in-person meeting and also people can call in via Zoom simultaneously, which is familiar from work and church settings for me.

    This type of hybrid meeting makes total sense to me because if someone doesn’t feel safe or they are traveling they can still participate. The hybrid model I heard described on the podcast makes very little sense to me because anyone with a health concern will have to be absent 25% of the time.

    Reply

    • Morgwn says

      November 19, 2021 at 6:41 am

      I suggested the same video conference solution as you stated above as “hybrid”, for those people who are still very vulnerable to Covid infections and not wanting to put themselves at risk with a big group meeting every month.

      To date, this solution of video conferencing in people from home as needed with the in-person meeting would satisfy both demands and I don’t see why we’re not offering it as an option to vote on. We have the technology available, so there’s no reason that we can’t use it to keep things safer against Covid and greener (i.e. not forcing everyone back into their cars needlessly).

      Reply

  2. Steven Herweyer says

    November 4, 2021 at 11:26 am

    Not a fan of Zoom at all. While it was necessary for a time, for many of us it was like having a brand new Yugo. Good in a pinch, but not good long term. For some members it is effective, but others not so much. When you leave the house for this important business meeting you are able to focus on the meeting. When you are on Zoom in your house and the baby is crying or your dogs need attention, you cannot just ignore them. Plus for some of us (financial advisors in particular), being seen and giving the handshake every week helps build the necessary trust to get people to give referrals where transactions are in the 10s of thousands of dollars. Also the pre-meeting networking in person is more effective. Plus it is hard to hear the reason from other members that they are concerned about Covid and then you find out that they are still taking the other business meetings in person, one wonders if Covid is the concern or if it is a 7 AM meeting that is the concern. Lastly, I wonder how the reaction to the BNI fee goes over with prospects when they find out they are getting a year (or two) of Zoom meetings for their fee. I know that live meetings have a higher cost and higher time commitment, but I think they in many cases are exponentially more effective. Respectfully, Steven Herweyer

    Reply

  3. Jeffrey Pasquale says

    November 4, 2021 at 2:59 pm

    We are BNI MainLine III – I am Jeffrey Pasquale the VP of this chapter…
    We have another option, that is what we are doing now and want to continue.
    Our chapter has been using this method from October to today. It is a real Hybrid option that we spent $$ on technology to get it to where it is today. It allows us to have remote users to be on line with Covid issues and not drop out as BNI Members. The system works and has been tested. I would like to invite one or all of our BNI Cooperate members to actually see what we are doing. We do not believe that our ambassador has relayed on what we are doing and how successful it has been.
    We are looking to add a 4th option or an a option. Or just for us to to the real hybrid. You can reach me on My mobile 6103041834 or email [email protected]

    Reply

  4. Edson Machado Moraes says

    November 4, 2021 at 3:45 pm

    Esse podcast nós ensina muito como podemos quebrar paradgmas trabalharmos nessa nova formação do hidrido, junto ao presencial online.
    Obrigado Dr Ivam ao Sam.

    Reply

  5. Ng Yoon Fatt says

    November 5, 2021 at 1:43 am

    I think hybrid is great, even 100% online is also great. My chapter in Malaysia, that is KL West – Alpha chapter, we opted to remain online. The chapter was launched online and we are staying online. Time has changed. Technology allows us to do things much simpler and efficient. We want to embrace that change.

    Reply

  6. SRIDHAR ALAMPALLI says

    November 5, 2021 at 7:57 am

    Due to sever covid, on line meetings were okay. Now we are all back to regular business meetings and covid is past in BANGALORE, India. Business has not grown for anybody as I have understood and in fact, it has declined. It is very important, that HYBRID meetings starts at the earliest. 51% is the majority wants hybrid meetings and only 16% only wants online meetings.

    Reply

  7. María Angélica López says

    November 12, 2021 at 4:10 pm

    Me gusta que las reuniones sean en línea, tenemos miembros del capitulo que no viven en la misma ciudad, así que en línea, si pueden participar. Ahorramos tiempo y dinero.

    Reply

  8. Vince Kassetas says

    January 12, 2022 at 10:39 am

    hello I just listened to episode 731 I am wondering if you have updated information on hybrid and zoom meetings. I mean info on polls you have taken with members about like and dislike of them since its been a year since this last info was taken.

    Reply

    • Ivan Misner says

      January 12, 2022 at 2:50 pm

      I did a special episode on the topic in November. It is Special Episode 5:

      https://www.bnipodcast.com/2021/11/29/special-episode-5-give-each-other-grace/

      Ivan

      Reply
